zh.lifeSummary: Reginald Muir of Polkelly, King of the Northern Isles and Argyll "having descended from the O'Moores of Ireland. The Muirs of Polkelly may have been the senior Muir line."是Somerled MacGillebride King of the Northern Isles的兒子，於1149年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾。他和Fonia Moray至少有1個兒子。他去世於1199年在蘇格蘭艾斯爾艾爾享年50歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

nameMeanings.name: Muir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived on a moor, from Older Scots muir ‘moor’ (an expanse of bare, wild upland country). There has been some confusion with Mure .

variants.name: Mure
meanings: Italian (Sicily): variant of   Morea  .
Scottish: variant of   Muir  .
English: occupational name from Old French muer ‘mewer’, i.e. someone who tended to hawks in the cages in which they were kept while moulting. Compare Mew .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: King
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English king ‘king’ (Old English cyning, cyng), perhaps acquired by someone with kingly qualities or as a pageant name by someone who had acted the part of a king or had been chosen as the master of ceremonies or ‘king’ of an event such as a tournament, festival or folk ritual. In North America, the surname King has absorbed several European cognates and equivalents with the same meaning, for example German König (see Koenig ) and Küng, French Roy , Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Kralj , Polish Krol . It is also very common among African Americans. It is also found as an artificial Jewish surname.
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name King, originally an Old English nickname from the vocabulary word cyning, cyng ‘king’.
Irish: adopted for a variety of names containing the syllable rí (which means ‘king’ in Irish).

variants.name: Kingdon
meanings: English (southwestern, especially Devon): habitational name from Higher Kingdon in Alverdiscott, or from Kendon in North Bovey, both in Devon. Both are named in Old English from cyning ‘king’ (see King ) or cyne- ‘royal’ + dūn ‘hill’.

variants.name: Chief
meanings: Native American: translation into English (and shortening) of a personal name based on a word, such as Cheyenne veho, meaning ‘chief’. See also King 4.

variants.name: Koenig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Dieter, Frieda, Monika, Reinhard, Egon, Fritz, Heinz, Wilhelm.

German (mainly König) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; also König): from Middle High German künic, German König ‘king’, hence a German nickname for a servant or retainer of a king (for example, a farmer on a royal demesne), or alternatively a status name for the head of a craftmen's guild, or a society of sharpshooters or minstrels. As a Jewish surname, it is artificial, one of several such Ashkenazic names based on European titles of nobility or royalty. The surname Koenig is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. In eastern Germany the surname König is in part a Germanized form (translation into German) of Sorbian Kral ‘king’. Similarly, in Czechia, Poland, Slovenia, and Croatia, it was often applied as a translation into German of Czech, Polish, Slovenian, and Croatian cognates Král (see Kral ), Król (see Krol ), and Kralj , respectively. Compare Konig .

variants.name: Kings
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of King , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 434, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vietnamese: unexplained.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄭, possibly based on its Hokkien or Teochew pronunciation, see   Zheng   1.

variants.name: Zheng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jian, Yi, Bin, Hong, Hua, Li, Wei, Yong, Ting, Xin, Yan, Hui, Min, Hao, Shen, Da, Quan, Tian, Chong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 鄭: from Zheng (鄭), the name of a state (located in part of present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) granted to You, the younger brother of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). The state of Zheng was annexed by the state of Han (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Northern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Midlands): from Middle English northern, northren ‘northern’ (Old English northerne), for someone ‘from the north’, particularly as an English nickname for someone from Scotland or Scandinavia, or a southern English nickname for someone from the north of the country.

variants.name: Northen
meanings: English: variant of Northern and Northend, the latter being derived from a lost place called Northend in Southowram (Yorkshire), or from one or more of the various places similarly named elsewhere, such as North End in Silsden (Yorkshire), Northend in Turville (Buckinghamshire), Northend in Ashwater (Devon), and Northend in Burton Dassett (Warwickshire). The placenames are derived from Middle English north ‘north, northern’ + ende ‘end, end of an estate, outlying part, outskirts’ (Old English north, ende).

nameMeanings.name: Isles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire) and Scottish (Angus and Perthshire): variant of   Iles   (see   Lyle  ).

variants.name: Iles
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Lisle   (see   Lyle  ).

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Isidor, Myer.

English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a maker of hoops and bands, etc., from Middle English band, bond, Middle High German, Middle Low German bant, German Band ‘hoop, metal band, fetter, shackle’, i.e. something used for tying or binding.
English: variant of   Bond  .

variants.name: Bandt
meanings: German: variant of   Band  .

variants.name: Bandstra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Wouter.
West Frisian: habitational name composed with Band 4 + the suffix -stra from Old Frisian sittera ‘inhabitant of’.

variants.name: Banta
meanings: West Frisian: probably a habitational name for someone from Bant, in the 17th century an island in Friesland, now a village north of Emmeloord in the Noordoostpolder (see   Band   4, compare   Banda   5). This surname is no longer found in the Netherlands.
History: Epke Jacobs Banta arrived in New Netherland in 1659.

variants.name: Bandel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Zvi, Emanuel, Hyman, Meir.
South German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from a diminutive of   Band  . See also   Bendel  .
German: in some cases, a variant of Pandel, a pet form of the medieval personal name Pantaleon (see Pantaleo ).

variants.name: Bend
meanings: English (Leicestershire and Nottinghamshire): from Middle English bend ‘band, fetter, chain; ribbon, sash, stripe’, for someone who made, wore, or used such items. See also the Band 1 and compare Bendy 1.

variants.name: Bandler
meanings: German: occupational name for a maker of band (for vessels) or ribbon or lace, from an agent derivative of Middle High German bandel, a diminutive of bant ‘band’ (see Band ).

variants.name: Bandy
meanings: English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandi, bondi, bendi, derivatives of Middle English and Old English band, bond, bend ‘band, ribbon, stripe’ + Middle English -i, reinforced by Old French bendé ‘striped’, with reference perhaps to clothing ornamented with ribbons or stripes, or to the diagonal band of a heraldic device. Compare Band .
English: alternatively, perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English bandy, bendy ‘curved stick’, used for someone with bent or crooked legs.
Czech and Slovak: from a derivative of banda ‘(gipsy) band’, apparently a nickname for a bandsman. This surname is found among Gypsies.

variants.name: Bond
meanings: English: status name for a peasant farmer or husbandman, Middle English bond(e), bounde, occasionally bande ‘bondman, customary tenant, serf’ (Old English bonda, bunda, reinforced by Old Norse bóndi). The Old Norse word was also in use as a personal name (Old Norse Bóndi, Bondi, Bundi, Bonde, borrowed as late Old English Bonda), and this has given rise to other English and Scandinavian surnames alongside those originating as status names, such as the Middle English personal name Bonde. The status of the peasant farmer fluctuated considerably during the Middle Ages; moreover, the underlying ancient Germanic word is of disputed origin and meaning. Among ancient Germanic peoples who settled to an agricultural life, the term came to signify a farmer holding lands from, and bound by loyalty to, a lord; from this developed the sense of a free landholder as opposed to a serf. In England after the Norman Conquest the word sank in status and became associated with the notion of bound servitude. The name can also be a variant of Band .
Swedish: variant of   Bonde  .
In some cases also an American shortened form of Ukrainian Bondarenko and possibly also of some other surname beginning with Bond-.

nameMeanings.name: Argyll
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English erguil(e), a variant of Middle English orguil(e), Old French orguel ‘pride, arrogance’, hence the variation of Argill and Argile with Orgill . Alternatively, perhaps a variant of Arkell , with voicing of k to g. No evidence has been found to support the idea that this is a habitational name from Argyll, the county of southwestern Scotland, though folk etymology to this effect may be partly responsible for the modern spelling of the surname.

variants.name: Orgill
meanings: English (Midlands): nickname from Middle English, Old French orgueil ‘pride’ (compare Pride ) or sometimes perhaps a post-medieval shortening of Orgiles, from the adjective orguilus ‘proud’. The French word may have been reinforced by the synonymous Middle English orgel (Old English orgol, orgel).

nameMeanings.name: Reginald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Norman origin, derived from Reginaldus, a Latinized form of Reynold influenced by Latin regina ‘queen’. The full form is now regarded as very formal, and bearers generally shorten it to Reg in ordinary usage.

en.lifeSummary: Reginald Muir of Polkelly, King of the Northern Isles and Argyll "having descended from the O'Moores of Ireland. The Muirs of Polkelly may have been the senior Muir line." was born in 1149, in Ayrshire, Scotland as the son of Somerled MacGillebride King of the Northern Isles. He had at least 1 son with Fonia Moray. He died in 1199, in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, at the age of 50, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Reginald Muir of Polkelly, King of the Northern Isles and Argyll "having descended from the O'Moores of Ireland. The Muirs of Polkelly may have been the senior Muir line." est né en 1149, à Ayrshire, Écosse, fils de Somerled MacGillebride King of the Northern Isles. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Fonia Moray. Il est décédé en 1199, à Ayr, Ayrshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Reginald Muir of Polkelly, King of the Northern Isles and Argyll "having descended from the O'Moores of Ireland. The Muirs of Polkelly may have been the senior Muir line." wurde 1149 in Ayrshire, Scotland, als Sohn von Somerled MacGillebride King of the Northern Isles geboren. Er hatte mit Fonia Moray mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1199 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Reginald Muir of Polkelly, King of the Northern Isles and Argyll "having descended from the O'Moores of Ireland. The Muirs of Polkelly may have been the senior Muir line." è nato il nel 1149 nel Ayrshire, Scotland come figlio di Somerled MacGillebride King of the Northern Isles. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Fonia Moray. Morì nel 1199, nel Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Reginald Muir of Polkelly, King of the Northern Isles and Argyll "having descended from the O'Moores of Ireland. The Muirs of Polkelly may have been the senior Muir line."は1149年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアでSomerled MacGillebride King of the Northern Islesの息子として生まれた。彼はFonia Morayとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1199年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアエイルで50歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Reginald Muir of Polkelly, King of the Northern Isles and Argyll "having descended from the O'Moores of Ireland. The Muirs of Polkelly may have been the senior Muir line."(은)는 1149년 스컷랜드 에이르실르에서 Somerled MacGillebride King of the Northern Isles의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Fonia Moray(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1199년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 에이르에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Reginald Muir of Polkelly, King of the Northern Isles and Argyll "having descended from the O'Moores of Ireland. The Muirs of Polkelly may have been the senior Muir line." nasceu em 1149, em Ayrshire, Scotland filho de Somerled MacGillebride King of the Northern Isles. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Fonia Moray. Ele faleceu em 1199, em Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Reginald Muir of Polkelly, King of the Northern Isles and Argyll "having descended from the O'Moores of Ireland. The Muirs of Polkelly may have been the senior Muir line." родился в 1149 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия. Его отца звали Somerled MacGillebride King of the Northern Isles. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Fonia Moray. Он умер в 1199 г. в местности аир, аиршир, Шотландия в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Reginald Muir of Polkelly, King of the Northern Isles and Argyll "having descended from the O'Moores of Ireland. The Muirs of Polkelly may have been the senior Muir line." nació en 1149, en Ayrshire, Scotland como hijo de Somerled MacGillebride King of the Northern Isles. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Fonia Moray. Murió en 1199, en Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Scotland.
